findings.title: LLM egress bypasses every policy layer
findings.expected: PRIVACY.md external_transmission=off and config/PERMISSIONS.md network=denied mean no LLM egress without per-call approval; raw claim must be scrub()'d before send.
findings.observed: zeref/cli.py:409-427 calls litellm.completion(model=gpt-4o-mini, messages=[{content: raw claim}]) unconditionally. No scrub, no SHARING_POLICY consult, no PERMISSIONS check, no approval prompt.
findings.impact: Silent egress of user claims to OpenAI when OPENAI_API_KEY set. Contradicts every policy doc.
findings.recommended_action: Gate cmd_grade behind SHARING_POLICY.connectors.<provider>.enabled + per-call approval + scrub of claim before send.

findings.title: Lineage subsystem makes unapproved GitHub API calls
findings.expected: SHARING_POLICY github.enabled=false + PERMISSIONS network=denied block outbound HTTP to api.github.com; per-call approval required.
findings.observed: zeref/lineage/importer.py:203-205, 292-310 calls urllib.request.urlopen('https://api.github.com/...') with Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN when env set. Fallback _github_json_via_gh (line 313) shells out to gh api on SSL failure — second bypass vector.
findings.impact: Repo metadata + user's PAT leave the machine outside any consent gate; SHARING_POLICY toggle is inert.
findings.recommended_action: Route every lineage call through a network-gate helper that reads SHARING_POLICY + PERMISSIONS; deny by default; prompt per URL.

findings.title: Internal absolute path leak in tracked config
findings.expected: REDACT.md internal_paths.enabled=true means /Users/<name>/... absolute paths never land in tracked files.
findings.observed: config/PROJECT.md:3 pins project_root to <REDACTED absolute path: /Users/<maintainer>/Documents/<vault>/00_SKILLS/zeref-os/.claude/worktrees/<slug>>. Reveals maintainer username, vault layout, foreign worktree slug.
verification: python3 -c 'from zeref.privacy import scrub, load_redact; s,r=scrub(open("config/PROJECT.md").read(), load_redact("REDACT.md"), provenance="audit"); print(r.redacted)'
findings.impact: Doxxes maintainer setup on public repo. Contradicts own privacy policy.
findings.recommended_action: Replace project_root value with relative marker or remove; add config/ to privacy-audit workflow scan target.

findings.title: Internal absolute path leak in public reference doc
findings.expected: references/ tree is public; must contain no /Users/<name>/ paths.
findings.observed: references/shared-anti-hallucination.md:89 contains /Users/<maintainer>/Documents/<vault>/01_REPOS/. references/ is in zeref/privacy.py:380 _SKIP set — scanner engineered to never see this file even in --strict mode.
findings.impact: Public reference doc leaks internal path; scanner blind by design.
findings.recommended_action: Redact the path in the doc; remove references/ from _SKIP or narrow to specific files.

findings.title: Privacy scanner scope excludes public trees + tracked config
findings.expected: Privacy audit scans all tracked surfaces likely to leak internal state.
findings.observed: zeref/privacy.py:380 _SKIP = {skills, _shared, agents, docs, references, team-packs, team}. zeref/privacy.py:383 rglob('*.md') — non-md files never scanned. .github/workflows/privacy-audit.yml:32 invokes cmd_audit_privacy with no --directory arg so cmd defaults to memory/ only (zeref/cli.py:440-465).
findings.impact: The two paths in ZRF-AUDIT-003/004 are undetectable by CI. Privacy claims fail external audit.
findings.recommended_action: Change default target to project root; scan .py/.json/.yml/.jsonl in addition to .md; keep _SKIP narrow (only tests/fixtures + docs/archive).

findings.title: config/PERMISSIONS.md never parsed at runtime
findings.expected: network: denied, mcp_servers: [], shell_commands allow/deny loaded and enforced.
findings.observed: grep 'PERMISSIONS' across zeref/ returns only zeref/release/doctor.py:38 (path existence check). No parser, no gate.
findings.impact: Policy file is documentation of intent, not an enforcement input. Setting network=denied has no runtime effect.
findings.recommended_action: Ship config/PERMISSIONS.md loader in zeref.privacy or zeref.security; every network/mcp/shell op consults it.

findings.title: SHARING_POLICY.md never parsed at runtime
findings.expected: Per-connector enabled/read_project_context/allowed_surfaces/redact_classes loaded and enforced.
findings.observed: grep 'SHARING_POLICY|sharing_policy' across zeref/ returns only zeref/release/doctor.py:28 (path existence check). Toggles are inert.
findings.impact: github.enabled=false does not block ZRF-AUDIT-002; every connector policy is spec-only.
findings.recommended_action: Ship SHARING_POLICY loader; every connector call (litellm, urlopen, gh, mcp) consults it.

findings.title: local-only mode is documentary-only
findings.expected: PRIVACY.md mode=local-only blocks writes to memory/sync/outbound/ and memory/sync/parent/.
findings.observed: grep 'local_only|local-only|sync/outbound|sync/parent' across zeref/ returns only directory-scaffolding strings (memory/core.py:45-46) and CLI arg-choice lists (cli.py:117,1091,1151). No guard.
findings.impact: Users trusting local-only for airgapped work get zero enforcement.
findings.recommended_action: Wire mode=local-only into MemoryWriter + sync commands; refuse writes to sync/outbound|sync/parent with clear error.

findings.title: Package uninstallable — pyproject build-backend id invalid
findings.expected: pip install . and pip install -e . succeed on the repo.
findings.observed: pyproject.toml:3 build-backend='setuptools.backends.legacy:build' — module does not exist in setuptools >=68. Correct is setuptools.build_meta. pip install fails with BackendUnavailable.
verification: cd $(mktemp -d) && python3 -m venv .v && . .v/bin/activate && pip install <repo> 2>&1 | grep BackendUnavailable
findings.impact: Every install path except manual PYTHONPATH is broken. v1.0.0 tag would ship an uninstallable package.
findings.recommended_action: Set build-backend='setuptools.build_meta' in pyproject.toml.

findings.title: zeref init produces a project the CLI cannot rediscover
findings.expected: zeref init scaffolds a project marker discover_project_root will find from any nested subdir.
findings.observed: zeref/memory/core.py:332 scaffold_project does not write AGENTS.md or REDACT.md. zeref/memory/core.py:299 discover_project_root uses AGENTS.md as the sole root marker. Nested zeref status prints 'PROJECT.md missing'.
verification: mkdir p && cd p && zeref init --name X --privacy abstract --tier standard --parent '' && cd .. && mkdir -p p/a/b && cd p/a/b && zeref status
findings.impact: Users cannot use zeref from any subdir of an init'd project.
findings.recommended_action: Either scaffold AGENTS.md in scaffold_project or change discover_project_root to a stable marker (config/PROJECT.md or a new .zeref file).

findings.title: ci.yml YAML malformed — whole workflow non-executing
findings.expected: PyYAML and GitHub Actions parser both accept ci.yml; SemVer tag guard + zeref-scope sweep run on every push.
findings.observed: .github/workflows/ci.yml:27-29 has 'with:' block dedented to same column as '- uses:' (col 7). PyYAML raises. Whole workflow silently non-executing; SemVer tag validator (:45-53) and zeref-scope sweep (:55-65) never run.
findings.impact: Tag guard + scope sweep dead. Any non-zeref file addition or malformed tag pushes without CI signal.
findings.recommended_action: Re-indent 'with:' block one level deeper (col 9) under '- uses:' at ci.yml:27-29.

findings.title: Lineage benchmarks fail-open on missing external CSV
findings.expected: ZRF_64_repo_lineage_intake.csv present; 10 lineage axes reproducible from clone.
findings.observed: zeref/lineage/importer.py:104-113 default_csv_path() looks for ZRF_64_repo_lineage_intake.csv in cwd + parent; neither location contains it. benchmarks/lineage_common.py:16-25 depends on it; 10 axes silently fail-open. benchmarks/run-all.py:239 writes results.json with 'passed: true' regardless.
findings.impact: 43% of published scores unreproducible from clone; downstream automation reads 'passed' when input was missing.
findings.recommended_action: Ship a public sample CSV (redacted) or gate lineage axes on CSV presence with explicit skip signal; results.json must record 'skipped' not 'passed'.

findings.title: Old trust audit auto-applied to new code
findings.expected: Verified trust score binds to a specific commit; recalculated when code changes.
findings.observed: benchmarks/run-all.py:81-117 _apply_verified_overrides reads docs/TRUST_AUDIT.md, regexes 'Verified score (this audit): N', and clamps trust axis to that number. Current value 9.70 dated 2026-06-19, no SHA field. Any future code change inherits 9.70.
findings.impact: Trust score is a permanent floor; audit-of-audit fails.
findings.recommended_action: Bind TRUST_AUDIT.md to a git SHA field; refuse override unless SHA matches current HEAD; else emit raw draft score.

findings.title: Lineage resolver fabricated — no real GitHub state observed
findings.expected: License, archived-flag, default-branch, commit-SHA come from real GitHub API.
findings.observed: benchmarks/lineage_common.py:41-72 _fake_resolver() invents commit_sha=f'fixture-{row.id:02d}', hardcodes default_branch='default', hardcodes one archived repo. 10 axes score against this fiction.
findings.impact: License/adoption/containment claims are performative — not empirical.
findings.recommended_action: Either publish real snapshot CSV with SHA + license fields, or rename axes as 'lineage-schema-conformance' with reduced claim scope.

findings.title: SOUL.md referenced as boot step 0 but absent
findings.expected: AGENTS.md §0 and CLAUDE.md §First-action mandate reading SOUL.md; file present at repo root.
findings.observed: ls SOUL.md → not found. Harness stubs (CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, GEMINI.md, LLAMA.md) do not mirror the reference; skip silently.
findings.impact: Boot step 0 fails on every session; agents skip SOUL principles.
findings.recommended_action: Create SOUL.md with the 5 operating principles, or remove the reference from AGENTS.md and update stubs to match.

findings.title: skill-importer orphaned from registry; Schema-B in Schema-A repo
findings.expected: Every skill on disk registered in zeref-registry.json; one frontmatter schema across skills.
findings.observed: skills/skill-importer/SKILL.md:1-16 self-declares registered; zeref-registry.json 14 entries do not include it. Skill-importer uses Schema-B keys (skill/triggers/deliverables/risk_level/model/model_alias) vs Schema-A (name/description/trigger/model/max_turns) used by 14 others.
verification: python3 -c "import json; r=json.load(open('zeref-registry.json')); print([s['skill'] for s in r['skills']])"
findings.impact: Router awareness broken; validator adds Schema-B mismatch risk on next registry entry.
findings.recommended_action: Register skill-importer with the Schema-B keys already in registry; or migrate to Schema-A + register.

findings.title: Registry has no agents[] or commands[] arrays
findings.expected: Single machine-readable source of truth per surface.
findings.observed: zeref-registry.json top-level keys are $schema/version/generated/skills only. 6 agents and 8 commands unregistered.
findings.impact: Harnesses cannot enumerate agents/commands programmatically; bidirectional consistency uncheckable.
findings.recommended_action: Add agents[] + commands[] arrays; add validator step; block on missing entries.

findings.title: Skill count contradiction across canonical docs
findings.expected: One count of skills, cited consistently.
findings.observed: AGENTS.md:122 says 14. AGENTS.md:255 says '10 disciplined skills'. skills/skill-router/SKILL.md:3,18,54 says 'all 10 skills' three times. INSTALL.md:83 says 'Skills: 10/10'. Registry: 14. Disk: 15.
verification: rg -n '10 skills|14 skills|15 skills' AGENTS.md INSTALL.md skills/
findings.impact: Router LLM misled at inference time; users trust wrong counts.
findings.recommended_action: Set canonical count = disk-derived (15); update AGENTS.md, INSTALL.md, skill-router SKILL.md; add CI check on skill count vs disk.

findings.title: Team pack count contradiction; taxonomy collision
findings.expected: One count, one schema for team packs.
findings.observed: AGENTS.md:212 = 6. docs/wiki/Home.md:30 = 9. docs/wiki/Architecture.md:5 = 6. Disk = 10 post-Phase-0.3 (9 pre). Two frontmatter schemas coexist: role packs (Schema A) + execution profiles (Schema B) + council (Schema B+). commands/team.md:3 argument-hint enumerates only 6 role packs.
findings.impact: 4 of 10 packs unvalidated + unsurfaced through /team; users cannot discover profiles or council.
findings.recommended_action: Split into team-packs/roles/ + team-packs/profiles/; add taxonomy discriminator field; extend commands/team.md argument-hint; extend validator to enumerate both.

findings.title: check-version-consistency.py does not compare against git tags
findings.expected: Version drift check catches tag-vs-VERSION divergence.
findings.observed: scripts/check-version-consistency.py compares 7 file surfaces against zeref/VERSION=1.0.0 — all match. Never inspects git tag list. Latest tags v2.6.1, v2.6.0 exceed VERSION by 6 minors.
findings.impact: Publish machinery may treat v2.6.1 as current; user-facing release-lineage looks inconsistent.
findings.recommended_action: Extend script to compare against latest tag; error if VERSION < latest tag major.minor; document intentional lineage-restart in docs/PIVOT_LOG.md.

findings.title: zeref release check is not a comprehensive gate
findings.expected: release check reruns tests, validator, version-consistency, package build, docs link, privacy audit, trust freshness, tag alignment.
findings.observed: zeref/release/checks.py runs 6 checks: version-file-exists, memory-layout, audit-log-presence, benchmark-verdict (no freshness), factguard-README, evidenceguard-docs. No pytest, no zeref-validate.py, no check-version-consistency.py, no package build, no plugin manifest lint, no link check, no privacy scan, no tag alignment.
findings.impact: Green release check while pip install broken (ZRF-AUDIT-009).
findings.recommended_action: Compose release check as sum of workflow gates; each subcheck emits SHA-bound evidence blob.

findings.title: HARNESS_MATRIX.md ✅ marks are self-attested
findings.expected: Every ✅ backed by host-observed boot log.
findings.observed: docs/HARNESS_MATRIX.md L38-47 claim scripts/harness-probe.py + manual smoke-test verify each host. Actual probe.py only file-checks stubs + shutil.which(binary). benchmarks/portability.py gives 10.0/10.0 on file-presence.
findings.impact: Public claim 'works across N harnesses' unsupported.
findings.recommended_action: Replace ✅ marks with evidence-state matrix per handoff (verified / partially / documented / unsupported / blocked); require host log for verified.

findings.title: zeref init is not truly non-interactive
findings.expected: Full flag set makes init headless.
findings.observed: Even with --name/--privacy/--tier, cmd_init prompts for 'Parent project path' unless --parent '' also supplied. EOFError under piped stdin.
verification: mkdir p && cd p && echo | zeref init --name X --privacy abstract --tier standard 2>&1
findings.impact: Breaks scripted install, CI, harness auto-bootstrap.
findings.recommended_action: Default --parent to '' (none) when other flags all supplied; only prompt if TTY.

findings.title: QUICKSTART.md labeled v2.5 with wrong install channel
findings.expected: Quickstart matches v1.0.0 install path.
findings.observed: QUICKSTART.md:1 labeled v2.5. QUICKSTART.md:10-11 says 'pipx install zeref-os' — not in current INSTALL.md.
findings.impact: New-user path wrong.
findings.recommended_action: Rewrite QUICKSTART.md to match INSTALL.md; strip v2.5 label.

findings.title: MIGRATION.md commands + validator filename stale
findings.expected: Migration commands executable at HEAD.
findings.observed: MIGRATION.md:65 uses 'claude plugin install zeref@zeref' — conflicts with INSTALL.md 'zeref-os@zeref-os'. MIGRATION.md:137 invokes scripts/zeref-validate-v4.py — file is scripts/zeref-validate.py.
findings.impact: Disaster-recovery path broken as documented.
findings.recommended_action: Update MIGRATION.md commands to current identifiers + filenames.

findings.title: Public issue templates lack security-redirect banner
findings.expected: Every public issue-picker entry warns against reporting security bugs publicly.
findings.observed: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md and feature_request.md have no redirect. Only security_note.yml + config.yml blank_issues_enabled:false partially close the vector.
findings.impact: Reporter picks 'Bug' and files sensitive info publicly.
findings.recommended_action: Prepend a 'DO NOT report security here → see SECURITY.md' block to both templates.

findings.title: Security fallback contact self-flagged placeholder; no PGP
findings.expected: Verified monitored email + PGP fingerprint published.
findings.observed: SECURITY_CONTACTS.md:9 'security+zeref-os@kanadhiayash.dev — (maintainer email — replace with the address you actually monitor)'. SECURITY_CONTACTS.md:10 'PGP fingerprint: to be published'.
findings.impact: No trustworthy out-of-band contact.
findings.recommended_action: Set + verify a monitored inbox; publish PGP fingerprint; remove placeholder comment.

findings.title: Repo identity drift inside .github/
findings.expected: Every .github/ URL points at the canonical repo (kanadhiayash/zeref-memory-engine).
findings.observed: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml:4,7,10 URLs point at github.com/kanadhiayash/zeref-os. SECURITY.md + security_note.yml point at zeref-memory-engine.
findings.impact: Reporter navigation lands in different projects; reduces trust in the private-report link (phishing-adjacent).
findings.recommended_action: Normalize every .github/ URL to kanadhiayash/zeref-memory-engine.

findings.title: Six axes miscategorized as behavioral
findings.expected: Axes named 'benchmark' measure runtime behavior.
findings.observed: adaptivity.py string-greps SKILL.md docs. scalability.py string-greps team-packs. portability.py majority file-presence. trust.py _score_test_suite comment 'we don't run coverage here (slow); we credit it via workflow existence'. token_efficiency.py enum-equality. public_claim_safety.py greps escalation string.
findings.impact: Public claims of 'benchmark verdict' inflate; external reviewer catches discrepancy.
findings.recommended_action: Rename conformance-only axes; require RUBRIC.md to bind each axis to its measurement class.

findings.title: Coverage floor at 15% under-signals regressions
findings.expected: Coverage floor tracks current coverage.
findings.observed: .github/workflows/test.yml:47 'coverage report -m --fail-under=15'.
findings.impact: Coverage can regress 80% without CI signal.
findings.recommended_action: Set floor to current actual coverage - 2%; ratchet up.

findings.title: Broken hero image URL on wiki front page
findings.expected: Home hero renders.
findings.observed: docs/wiki/Home.md:3 hero URL points at raw.githubusercontent.com/kanadhiayash/zeref-os/... — canonical repo is zeref-memory-engine.
findings.impact: First-impression failure on wiki.
findings.recommended_action: Rewrite URL to zeref-memory-engine; or drop image dependency.

findings.title: Docs pre-announce v1.1 while CHANGELOG tops at v1.0.0
findings.expected: Active surfaces cite shipped version.
findings.observed: docs/HARDENING_OVERVIEW.md:1 labeled v1.1. CHANGELOG.md:8 tops at [1.0.0].
findings.impact: Users see contradictory version signals.
findings.recommended_action: Label doc as roadmap/proposal; move to docs/plans/ or annotate 'planned'.

findings.title: Duplicate Inspirations doc
findings.expected: One canonical Inspirations page.
findings.observed: docs/archive/INSPIRATIONS.md + docs/wiki/Inspirations.md coexist with drift.
findings.impact: Confuses future doc archaeologists.
findings.recommended_action: Merge into docs/wiki/Inspirations.md; archive-mark the docs/archive/ copy or delete.

findings.title: Wiki-style [[Page]] links break outside GitHub Wiki UI
findings.expected: Links resolve in both wiki and file-tree browsing.
findings.observed: docs/wiki/_Sidebar.md:3+, docs/wiki/Memory-Model.md:160 use [[...]]. Only render on GitHub Wiki host.
findings.impact: Broken navigation in IDE previews.
findings.recommended_action: Convert to relative markdown links; keep [[]] only in Sidebar.md if wiki-only.

findings.title: Stale Notion 'Zeref-Agent-OS-Command-Center' link
findings.expected: External links match current identity.
findings.observed: CHANGELOG.md:207 + docs/RELEASE_LOG.md:29 reference the old Notion.
findings.impact: Stale attribution.
findings.recommended_action: Update or remove.

findings.title: 'God Mode' tier terminology retained without deprecation notice
findings.expected: Public README + wiki use one tier vocabulary.
findings.observed: AGENTS.md:45, commands/start.md:20, commands/status.md:18 use 'God Mode'. README + wiki omit tier vocabulary.
findings.impact: Tier vocabulary drifts.
findings.recommended_action: Rename to 'Enterprise' (matches team-packs/enterprise.md) or document as intentional and cite in README.

findings.title: branch-retention.yml comment vs triggers mismatch
findings.expected: Comment matches triggers.
findings.observed: .github/workflows/branch-retention.yml:4 comment 'push to main + PR close'; actual triggers L8-10 are delete + workflow_dispatch only.
findings.impact: Doc drift.
findings.recommended_action: Rewrite comment to describe delete-hook + manual dispatch.

findings.title: run-all.py docstring 'no axis below 8.0' contradicts pass-gate ≥9.0
findings.expected: Docstring matches code.
findings.observed: benchmarks/run-all.py:7-8 docstring 'exit 0 iff every axis ≥ 9.0 and no axis < 8.0'. Line 234 passed = not failures; collect_failures treats < 9.0 as failure. 8.0 tolerance band is dead.
findings.impact: Minor spec drift.
findings.recommended_action: Rewrite docstring; or introduce the 8.0 soft-warn band the docstring promised.

findings.title: skills/drafts/ contract without filesystem presence
findings.expected: Path exists or references are removed.
findings.observed: Directory absent; referenced by AGENTS.md:46, scripts/zeref-validate.py:236-243, commands/review-skill.md:9, agents/pattern-observer.md, skills/pattern-to-skill/, skills/skill-importer/. Validator handles absence gracefully but /review-skill and pattern-to-skill assume presence.
findings.impact: Silent workflow no-op on fresh install.
findings.recommended_action: Create skills/drafts/.gitkeep + README; or remove references.

findings.title: Python-version classifier vs requires-python mismatch
findings.expected: Classifiers reflect declared version range.
findings.observed: pyproject.toml:9 requires-python='>=3.11'; classifiers L11-19 list only 3.11 and 3.12.
findings.impact: 3.13/3.14 users get no classifier signal.
findings.recommended_action: Add 3.13, 3.14 classifiers; test in CI matrix.
